lesson.menu_desc: Asking for and following directions with imperatives -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Getting Around Washington -- Directions (Go straight / Turn left / imperatives)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 14 -- Getting Around Washington

listenings.text: Sure, I can help you get to the conference center. It is not far, about a ten minute walk. From the hotel, go straight down this street for two blocks. At the second traffic light, turn left onto K Street. Walk past the coffee shop and the bank. You will see a small park on the corner. The conference center is the tall glass building right across from the park, next to the subway station. If you reach the museum, you have gone too far, so just turn around. You really cannot miss it.

Excuse me, I am trying to find the National Gallery. Let me see if I understood your directions. So, I go straight down this avenue for three blocks, then I cross the road at the crosswalk near the big landmark. After that, I turn right at the corner and the gallery is the building between the library and the cafe. Is that correct? Thank you so much for your help. I really appreciate it. I think I can find it now.
